behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between file parsing, env override merge, and validation/error formatting. A unit test of validator-only logic can miss path-shape or precedence bugs introduced during module wiring. No real subprocess/network/OS primitive is required beyond local in-process setup.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp config file missing `database.host`, clear corresponding `TOOL_...` env var, run the loader entrypoint in-process, and assert the raised startup error includes exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module behavior (env read + path resolution + loader open target). A pure unit path helper test could miss loader wiring errors. System rejected because behavior text does not require subprocess or OS-only effects; temp filesystem plus env injection is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory, place config only at `<xdg>/tool/config.yaml`, ensure `~/.config/tool/config.yaml` is absent, run loader, and assert it loads from the XDG path.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundary: file values, env decoding, and precedence merge. Unit-testing a merge helper alone may miss key-mapping/normalization mismatch from env (`TOOL_...`) to nested config path. No System/Agentic factors.
behaviors.test_description: Write config with `database.host: file-host`, set matching `TOOL_...` env var to `env-host`, run full loader in-process, and assert final config uses `env-host`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Catching real failure requires real YAML parser exception propagation through loader error handling. Mocking parser exceptions can hide incompatibilities in actual exception fields/messages. System rejected: no subprocess/network needed; local file + real parser in-process is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Create malformed YAML at a known line in temp config, run loader, assert startup error includes that line number from parser output.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is filesystem side-effect orchestration within process (existence check -> mkdir -> write defaults -> continue load). This needs real local FS interaction across modules, but not subprocess/OS eventing beyond normal file I/O, so System is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Point config root to a non-existent temp path, run startup loader, assert directory now exists, defaults file was written with expected baseline keys, and loader proceeds without fatal error.
